names.scientific_name: Sodium carbonate (E500i), Sodium hydrogen carbonate/bicarbonate (E500ii), Sodium sesquicarbonate (E500iii)

classification.sub_class: raising agent, alkalizing agent, anti-caking agent

natural_occurrence.description: Sodium carbonate occurs naturally in the mineral trona and in certain alkaline lake deposits. However, most commercial sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate are synthetically manufactured through the Solvay process or extracted from trona ore.

manufacturing.description: E500i (sodium carbonate) is primarily produced via the Solvay process (reacting sodium chloride with ammonia and carbon dioxide) or by mining and processing natural trona ore. E500ii (sodium bicarbonate/baking soda) is made by passing CO2 through a solution of sodium carbonate, or directly from trona. E500iii (sesquicarbonate) is a mixed crystal of sodium carbonate and bicarbonate, also derived from trona processing.

other_uses.cosmetics: pH adjuster in soaps, bath products, and personal care items.
other_uses.industrial: Glass manufacturing, water softening, detergents, pulp and paper production.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Antacid (sodium bicarbonate), pH buffer in medications.
other_uses.household: Baking soda for cleaning, deodorizing, and home remedies; washing soda for laundry.

eu.conditions: Permitted in most food categories at levels necessary to achieve the intended technical effect.

usa.notes: Sodium bicarbonate is one of the most widely used leavening agents in baking.

japan.notes: Widely used in Japanese confectionery and noodle production.

safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: not specified (no safety concern at current use levels)

commonly_found_in: Baking powder and baking soda (E500ii is THE baking soda)
Breads, cakes, cookies, and all baked goods
fun_facts: E500ii (sodium bicarbonate) IS baking soda - one of the most iconic kitchen ingredients worldwide
When mixed with acid (like vinegar or lemon juice), baking soda produces CO2 bubbles - the classic science fair volcano reaction
Used for over 150 years in home baking, cleaning, and even as a folk remedy for heartburn
The 'bicarb' in sodium bicarbonate means it has two carbonate groups, making it a perfect CO2 generator for leavening
Arm & Hammer has been selling baking soda since 1867, making it one of America's oldest branded products
Can be used to tenderize meat, reduce acidity in tomato sauce, and even clean teeth - a true kitchen multitasker
